scenes.description: Man aged 62-68, suburban driveway, morning light. He holds a large gas leaf blower with both hands, body slightly leaning forward, one shoulder visibly lower from the weight. Expression is not dramatic — just tired, resigned. Wearing a flannel shirt and work pants. Fallen leaves around him. No exaggerated pain, no theatrical grimace — the exhaustion is subtle and credible. Background: a well-kept ranch-style house, overcast natural morning light.
scenes.mood: Authentic, sober, quietly heavy — the visual equivalent of a sigh. No staging, no stock photo feel.

scenes.description: Horizontal split screen. LEFT: cluttered garage wall — gas blower hanging crooked, tangled orange extension cord coiled on the floor, ice scrapers, a chamois bundle, a rake with a broken handle, dusty shelves. The chaos conveys years of accumulation and avoidance. RIGHT: the exact same wall angle, now clean and minimal — only the Seese Pro in its compact carrying case, hung or placed neatly. White wall, one hook, one tool. The contrast is about mental relief as much as physical order.
scenes.mood: Contrast between visual noise and visual calm. The right side should feel like an exhale.

scenes.description: Clean editorial product shot on a pure white or very light warm-gray background. The Seese Pro is centered, positioned upright at a slight 15-degree angle. To its immediate left: a standard 1-liter water bottle for scale reference. Around the product, four typographic stat callouts in a minimal editorial style (no icons, no gradients): '950g', '200+ MPH', '30,000 RPM', '5 min / 400 sq ft'. Typography: clean sans-serif, dark on light. No background texture, no props beyond the water bottle.
scenes.mood: Precise, confident, editorial. The visual language of a serious product review, not an ad.

scenes.description: Triptych: three separate portrait-format panels placed side by side. Panel 1: a man, late 60s, standing on a brick patio, holding the Seese Pro loosely at his side, looking slightly off-camera with a small satisfied smile. Panel 2: a woman, early 70s, near her garage door, Seese Pro in one hand, the other resting on the door frame — relaxed, grounded posture. Panel 3: a man, mid-60s, beside a clean sedan in a driveway, Seese Pro hanging at his side. All three: natural outdoor light, no forced smiles, genuine ease in the body language. Diverse but cohesive in tone.
scenes.mood: Quiet pride, normalcy, independence. Real people, real homes — zero stock photo artificiality.

scenes.description: Side-by-side editorial split. LEFT PANEL: person aged 62-68, struggling posture, two hands gripping a large gas blower on a leaf-covered driveway — shoulders tense, jaw slightly set. Warm but slightly desaturated color grade to reinforce heaviness. RIGHT PANEL: same demographic (different person acceptable), holding the Seese Pro with one relaxed hand at the side, upright natural posture, clean driveway behind them. Looking forward calmly. Brighter, warmer grade on the right. Both panels shot at the same time of day, same residential setting.
scenes.mood: The left is effort and resignation. The right is ease and quiet competence. The contrast should read instantly.

scenes.description: Flat lay on a clean, warm light-gray or off-white surface. The open compact carrying case is centered, slightly angled. Inside: the Seese Pro neatly placed. Around the case, arranged with breathing room: the 21V 3.0Ah battery, the rapid charger, and the 3 bonus accessories (nozzle attachments). Everything is organized, purposeful — no overlap, no clutter. Soft even studio light, slight shadow to add depth. The presentation conveys: 'complete, professional, ready to use today.'
scenes.mood: Premium without pretension. Calm confidence. The visual equivalent of an unboxing that delivers on its promise.

scenes.description: Woman aged 60-68, standing at the edge of a freshly cleared driveway or stone patio. She holds the Seese Pro with one hand at her side, arm relaxed, not posing — as if she just finished and paused for a moment. Upright posture, feet slightly apart. She looks forward or toward the clean space she just cleared — not at the camera. Expression: calm satisfaction, not a grin. Morning light, golden hour warmth. Well-kept residential exterior behind her. The image should read: 'I did this. Myself. And it was easy.'
scenes.mood: Dignity, autonomy, quiet pride. Warm but never saccharine. The definitive emotional payoff of the advertorial.
